title: hypericum terrae-firmae
intro: Hypericum terrae-firmae, also known as St. John's wort, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, known for its yellow flowers that bloom in mid-to-late summer. The plant contains bioactive compounds such as hypericin and hyperforin, which have been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of St. John's wort include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful for treating anxiety, depression, and sleep disorders, as well as wound healing and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of St. John's wort include teas, tinctures, and ointments, which can be consumed or applied topically to reap its health benefits.
